file_insights.purpose: Excludes files and directories from Docker build context to reduce image size
key_patterns: Excludes version control (.git, .gitignore)
Excludes Python artifacts (__pycache__, *.pyc)
Excludes virtual environments (venv/, env/)
Excludes IDE files (.idea/, .vscode/)
Excludes environment files (.env, .env.local) but preserves .env.example
Excludes test and coverage files
Excludes development/documentation files (docs/, *.md except README.md)
Excludes Docker files from context recursion
Excludes CI/CD configuration files
file_insights.notable_decisions: Explicitly preserves .env.example while excluding actual .env files for security

file_insights.purpose: Multi-stage Docker build configuration for production, development, Celery worker, and Celery beat deployments
key_patterns: Stage 1 (builder): Python 3.11-slim base with build dependencies
Creates virtual environment in /opt/venv with pip upgrades
Stage 2 (production): Minimal runtime image with only runtime dependencies
Non-root user creation (UID/GID 1000) for security
Dynamic environment variable configuration for Gunicorn
Entrypoint script with database connection retry logic
Static file collection during build with dummy credentials
Health check using curl
Tini as init system for proper signal handling
Gunicorn configuration with gthread worker class and shared memory optimization
Stage 3 (development): Extended production image with dev dependencies and aliases
Stage 4 (celery-worker): Celery worker configuration with prefork pool
Stage 5 (celery-beat): Celery beat scheduler for periodic tasks
notable_decisions: Multi-stage build reduces final image size by separating build dependencies
Uses python:3.11-slim-bookworm as base for smaller footprint
Implements database readiness check in entrypoint script
Uses shared memory (/dev/shm) for Gunicorn worker temp directory
Sets DJANGO_MIGRATE env var to control migration execution
Includes forwarded-allow-ips configuration for reverse proxy support
Development stage reuses production image rather than creating separate base

patterns_discovered.description: Uses 5 distinct stages (builder, production, development, celery-worker, celery-beat) to create optimized images for different deployment scenarios
patterns_discovered.rationale: Reduces image size by separating build dependencies from runtime, allows reuse across different service types

patterns_discovered.description: Non-root user creation with fixed UID/GID, minimal attack surface with slim Python base, explicit secret key handling
patterns_discovered.rationale: Follows container security best practices to prevent privilege escalation and unauthorized access

patterns_discovered.description: Comprehensive use of ENV for Gunicorn settings, Django settings, application paths, and runtime options
patterns_discovered.rationale: Enables flexible deployment across environments (dev, staging, production) without rebuilding image

patterns_discovered.description: Detailed .dockerignore file covering Python, IDE, environment, test, and CI/CD files
patterns_discovered.rationale: Minimizes build context size and prevents accidental inclusion of sensitive files

patterns_discovered.description: Entrypoint script checks database readiness before starting application, with configurable retry logic
patterns_discovered.rationale: Ensures database is available before migrations and application startup in distributed systems

patterns_discovered.description: Uses tini as init system to properly forward SIGTERM/SIGKILL signals
patterns_discovered.rationale: Critical for graceful shutdown and proper cleanup in containerized environments

gotchas_discovered.description: Uses placeholder DATABASE_URL and SECRET_KEY during collectstatic - these are not used at runtime
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Comment explains this is build-time only; actual credentials come from runtime environment

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Database readiness depends on dj_database_url availability
gotchas_discovered.description: Entrypoint script imports dj_database_url which must be in requirements.txt
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should verify dj_database_url is included in project dependencies

gotchas_discovered.description: HEALTHCHECK command curls /health/ endpoint which may not be implemented in Django app
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should implement health check endpoint in Django or modify HEALTHCHECK command

gotchas_discovered.description: Default GUNICORN_WORKERS=3 and THREADS=2 may not be optimal for all deployments
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Environment variables allow easy tuning per deployment; should document recommended values

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Development stage command differs from production
gotchas_discovered.description: Development stage uses 'runserver' while production uses 'gunicorn', breaking image inheritance if not careful
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documentation should clarify stage selection (docker build --target=development)

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Celery worker and beat stages share base without explicit config
gotchas_discovered.description: Celery configuration must be present in config module; no fallback if missing
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Assumes config.settings has Celery configuration defined

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a comprehensive, production-ready multi-stage Docker setup with excellent separation of concerns
key_accomplishments: Implemented 5-stage build supporting production, development, and Celery workloads
Security hardening with non-root user, minimal base image, and proper signal handling
Database readiness checking and migration automation via entrypoint script
Flexible configuration via environment variables for multi-environment deployment
Health checks and graceful shutdown support for orchestration platforms

recommendations.rationale: Entrypoint script depends on this package for database readiness check

recommendations.recommendation: Implement /health/ endpoint in Django for HEALTHCHECK command
recommendations.rationale: Current health check assumes endpoint exists; should either implement or modify check

recommendations.recommendation: Document recommended Gunicorn worker configuration for different workload types
recommendations.rationale: Default settings (3 workers, 2 threads) may not be optimal; document tuning guidance

recommendations.recommendation: Document Docker build usage examples (e.g., docker build --target=development)
recommendations.rationale: Multiple stages available but users need guidance on when to use each

recommendations.recommendation: Add optional APK packages for Alpine support if space-constrained
recommendations.rationale: Current debian-based slim image is ~150MB; Alpine could reduce to ~50MB if needed

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding development stage with debugpy for remote debugging
recommendations.rationale: Would enhance development experience for containerized local development

recommendations.recommendation: Document environment variable defaults and recommended values for production
recommendations.rationale: GUNICORN_WORKERS, TIMEOUT, and other settings should have documented guidance
